title: The Porcupine and the Snakes
lines: A Porcupine sought shelter in a cave during a storm.
He found it already occupied by Snakes.
'Please, let me share your shelter," begged the Porcupine.
The Snakes agreed, and the Porcupine settled in.
But soon his quills began to prick the Snakes.
'Your quills hurt us," complained the Snakes.
'If you do not like it," replied the Porcupine, 'you are free to leave.'
moral: Some things cannot be changed; learn to accept them or remove yourself.
